questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If the following equations has to be balance, then the signs of which of the following options will be used? 24 ÷ 12 × 16 = 0

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which one of the given interchanges in signs would make the given equation correct? 10 - 2 + 9 × 2 + 4 = 19

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which one of the given interchanges in signs and numbers would make the given equation correct? 16 - 8 × 16 - 2 + 4 + 3 = 15

solutions.solution: Let us check all the options one-by-one. From option (a), 24 - 6 + 12 + 16 = 0 ⇒ (24 + 12 + 16) - 6 = 0 ⇒ 52 - 6 = 0 ⇒ 46 = 0 ⇒ LHS ≠ RHS Hence, option (a) is incorrect. From option (b), 24 + 6 + 12 + 16 = 0 ⇒ 4 + 0.75 = 0 ⇒ 4.75 = 0 ⇒ LHS ≠ RHS Hence, option (b) is incorrect. From option (c), 24 - 6 - 12 - 16 = 0 ⇒ 18 - 12 - 16 = 0 ⇒ 6 - 16 = 0 ⇒ -10 = 0 ⇒ LHS ≠ RHS Hence, option (c) is incorrect. From option (d), 24 ÷ 6 + 12 - 16 = 0 ⇒ 24/6 + 12 - 16 = 0 ⇒ 4 + 12 - 16 = 0 ⇒ 16 - 16 = 0 ⇒ 0 = 0 ⇒ LHS = RHS Hence, option (d) is correct.

Let us check the options one-by-one. From option (a), 10 - 2 + 9 × 2 + 4 = 19 ⇒ 10 + 2 + 9 × 2 - 4 = 19 ⇒ 5 + 9 × 2 - 4 = 19 ⇒ 5 + 18 - 4 = 19 ⇒ 23 - 4 = 19 ∴ 19 = 19 As option (a) gives us the correct answer. Hence, there is no need to check other options.
